evals.prompt: Run a tick that finds a LinkedIn draft scheduled for now: `python3 scripts/tick.py` against a draft with channel=linkedin, posting_status=scheduled, scheduled_at <= now(IST).
evals.expected_output: Tick acquires lock, dispatches to publishers/linkedin.py (autonomous API), updates frontmatter on success: posted_at, platform_url, posting_status: posted. Moves file from pending-approval/ to published/. Logs to scheduler.log.

assertions.name: lock_acquired
assertions.description: fcntl flock acquired on /tmp/digischola-scheduler.lock before scanning

assertions.name: linkedin_dispatch
assertions.description: Draft routed to publishers/linkedin.py based on channel field

assertions.name: frontmatter_updated
assertions.description: On success: posted_at, platform_url, posting_status: posted all set

assertions.name: file_moved
assertions.description: Draft moved from pending-approval/ to published/

assertions.name: log_entry
assertions.description: scheduler.log gains a 'posted' entry with draft id, channel, url

evals.prompt: Run a tick where the LinkedIn API returns HTTP 426 NONEXISTENT_VERSION on first attempt, then succeeds on retry.
evals.expected_output: First attempt fails. posting_attempts incremented. Tick exits without moving file. After retry-after delay (5s for attempt 2), tick re-fires and either succeeds or escalates per backoff schedule (5s → 30s → 2min → permanent fail at attempt 4).

assertions.name: retry_backoff_respected
assertions.description: Wait times follow 5s, 30s, 2min schedule between attempts

assertions.name: permanent_fail_at_4
assertions.description: After 4 failed attempts, posting_status set to failed and notification fired

assertions.name: no_double_post
assertions.description: Lock prevents concurrent ticks from re-attempting in parallel

assertions.name: frontmatter_attempts_count
assertions.description: posting_attempts field tracks the retry count across tick invocations
